info.name: P21 AP Reconciliation
info.description:
API calls for AP statement reconciliation.

Before running:
1. Set collection variables: P21_BASE_URL, P21_USERNAME, P21_PASSWORD, SUPPLIER_ID
2. Run '1. Get Token' first — saves token to P21_TOKEN automatically
3. Confirmed AP table: apinv_hdr
   Key columns: vendor_id (integer), invoice_no, invoice_amount, voucher_type (V=invoice/D=credit memo), reverse_flag (Y=voided)

variable.description: P21 server base URL, no trailing slash
Filled automatically by the 'Get Token' request
P21 vendor_id (integer, no quotes) — e.g. 100050 for Burten Distribution
Earliest invoice date from the vendor statement (ISO 8601 UTC)

exec: console.log('Token saved. Expires in ' + json.ExpiresIn + ' seconds.');

request.description:
Obtain a Bearer JWT (24-hour TTL). Token is saved automatically to P21_TOKEN.

NOTE: Content-Length: 0 header is mandatory — server returns 411 without it.
Validates an existing token. Returns {"IsValid": true} or {"IsValid": false}. Use GET (not POST).

Probe the confirmed AP voucher table. Returns one row so you can verify column names.

Confirmed columns:
- vendor_id (integer)
- invoice_no (string)
- invoice_amount (decimal; negative = credit memo)
- voucher_type ('V' = invoice, 'D' = debit/credit memo)
- reverse_flag ('Y' = voided — exclude these)
- invoice_date (datetime)
- net_due_date, paid_in_full, po_no, description

item.name: 4. Get AP Vouchers for a Supplier (with date filter)

url.raw: {{P21_BASE_URL}}/odataservice/odata/table/apinv_hdr?$filter=vendor_id eq {{SUPPLIER_ID}} and reverse_flag ne 'Y' and invoice_date ge {{MIN_INVOICE_DATE}}&$select=invoice_no,vendor_id,invoice_amount,voucher_type,reverse_flag,invoice_date,net_due_date,paid_in_full,po_no&$top=50

query.value: vendor_id eq {{SUPPLIER_ID}} and reverse_flag ne 'Y' and invoice_date ge {{MIN_INVOICE_DATE}}

request.description:
Fetch AP vouchers for a supplier filtered by date.

Set before running:
- SUPPLIER_ID: integer vendor_id (no quotes) — e.g. 100050
- MIN_INVOICE_DATE: earliest invoice date from the statement — e.g. 2024-01-01T00:00:00Z

IMPORTANT: vendor_id is an integer column — do NOT wrap {{SUPPLIER_ID}} in quotes.

voucher_type values: 'V' = regular invoice, 'D' = debit/credit memo
reverse_flag = 'Y' means voided — excluded by the filter.

item.name: 5. Look Up Single Invoice by Invoice Number

url.raw: {{P21_BASE_URL}}/odataservice/odata/table/apinv_hdr?$filter=vendor_id eq {{SUPPLIER_ID}} and invoice_no eq 'INV-NUMBER-HERE' and invoice_date ge {{MIN_INVOICE_DATE}}&$select=invoice_no,vendor_id,invoice_amount,voucher_type,reverse_flag,invoice_date,net_due_date,paid_in_full,po_no

query.value: vendor_id eq {{SUPPLIER_ID}} and invoice_no eq 'INV-NUMBER-HERE' and invoice_date ge {{MIN_INVOICE_DATE}}

request.description:
Look up one specific invoice for a supplier.

Replace 'INV-NUMBER-HERE' with an actual invoice number from a vendor statement.
Set SUPPLIER_ID and MIN_INVOICE_DATE as collection variables.

This tests the exact match logic reconcile.py will use.
